message:
perf(btree): gate Instant::now() behind copy-profile flag

Hot-path instrumentation recorders previously captured a timestamp with
std::time::Instant::now() unconditionally at every call site, then
checked the copy_profile_enabled() gate inside the recorder. When the
gate was off (the normal case) that still cost two clock_gettime
syscalls per op for code paths that never consumed the duration.

Changes:
- Introduce instrumentation::profile_start() that returns
  Some(Instant::now()) only when the gate is on, else None.
- Refactor record_fast_table_leaf_{payload,full_cell}_append_{mutate,stage},
  record_quick_balance_attempt, record_local_split_attempt (and
  similar) to take Option<Instant> sentinels; the recorder computes
  elapsed_ns via profile_elapsed_ns() only when a timestamp was
  captured.
- Update cursor.rs call sites to pass profile_start() tokens through
  the mutate/stage pairs.

Net effect: zero clock_gettime on profile-disabled hot paths, identical
behavior when the flag is enabled. No semantic change to recorded
metrics.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
